steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the paragraph. The paragraph describes two prior research directions on convergent evolution, identifies what remains unknown between them, and then explains what motivated Green and Extavour's study. The purpose must reflect how that structure works as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: the paragraph does not prescribe what researchers should study — it describes context for one specific study. ✗
  • Choice B: the paragraph does not present a new problem or open question for readers to solve. ✗
  • Choice C — 'situate Green and Extavour's study in the context of their field': the paragraph frames their work within two prior research directions and identifies the gap that motivated it — exactly what situating a study in its field means. ✓
  • Choice D: the paragraph does not criticize or challenge prior research — it simply maps the landscape. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
